cubes.name: Preparation for Fixing a Leaking Faucet

rules.rule: Turn off the water supply valves under the sink by twisting them clockwise until tight.
Plug the drain with a rag or stopper to prevent small parts from falling in.
Gather tools including adjustable wrench, screwdriver set, replacement washers or O-rings, and plumber's grease.
Identify the faucet type (compression, ball, cartridge, or ceramic disk) by checking the handle mechanism.
Do not proceed without turning off the water to avoid flooding.
Avoid using excessive force on handles or nuts to prevent stripping threads.
Wear safety gloves to protect hands from sharp edges or old grime.
Ensure the area is well-lit; use a flashlight if needed to avoid mistakes.
Lay down a towel under the faucet to catch any drips and protect the sink surface.
Do not ignore leaks from the supply lines; address them separately if present.

trigger_intent: how to prepare to fix a dripping faucet
tools needed for faucet leak repair
safety prep for fixing sink faucet
turn off water for faucet fix
cubes.rationale: Proper preparation ensures safety and prevents further damage during faucet repair. Identifying the faucet type and gathering tools streamlines the process, reducing frustration and errors. This approach, sourced from WikiHow, emphasizes efficiency and precaution to achieve a successful fix.

cubes.name: Fixing a Compression Faucet Leak

rules.rule: Remove the handle by unscrewing the set screw with a screwdriver and lifting it off.
Use an adjustable wrench to loosen and remove the packing nut.
Pull out the stem and inspect the rubber washer at the bottom; replace if worn.
Apply plumber's grease to the new washer and O-ring before reassembling.
Reassemble in reverse order and test by turning on the water slowly.
Do not overtighten the packing nut to avoid damaging the stem.
Avoid using metal tools directly on chrome surfaces; wrap with tape to prevent scratches.
Be cautious of old parts that may be corroded and break easily.
If the seat is damaged, use a seat wrench to replace it, but only if experienced.
Clean all parts with a soft cloth and vinegar to remove mineral buildup.
Do not force the stem back in if it doesn't fit smoothly; check for obstructions.

trigger_intent: stem and washer fix in sink
common errors in faucet stem repair
cubes.rationale: Compression faucets leak due to worn washers, and replacing them is a straightforward fix. This method from WikiHow provides detailed steps to disassemble, inspect, and reassemble safely. Following these rules prevents common pitfalls like overtightening, ensuring a long-lasting repair.

cubes.name: Fixing a Cartridge or Ball Faucet Leak

rules.rule: Pry off the decorative cap on the handle and remove the screw underneath with a screwdriver.
Lift off the handle and use pliers to remove the retaining clip or nut.
Pull out the cartridge or ball assembly and inspect O-rings and seats for wear; replace as needed.
Lubricate new parts with plumber's grease and align properly during reinsertion.
Reassemble and turn on water to check for leaks, adjusting the cam or cap if necessary.
Do not mix parts from different faucet brands; use exact replacements.
Avoid bending or damaging the retaining clip during removal.
Water may spurt out when removing the cartridge; have a towel ready.
If the leak persists after repair, the entire faucet may need replacement.
For ball faucets, ensure the ball is seated correctly in the valve body.
Do not use excessive force on plastic cartridges to prevent cracking.

trigger_intent: avoid damage in faucet cartridge fix
cubes.rationale: Cartridge and ball faucets require specific disassembly to access internal seals. WikiHow's guidance ensures users replace worn components accurately without causing further issues. This structured approach minimizes water waste and promotes DIY competence for common household problems.
